codeReview.summary: The commit does add a polling-safe session bootstrap helper and updates workspace-switch polls to use it, which addresses the reported transient 500 failure mode. However, the same commit also rewrites the existing VAL-CROSS-005 timer-page test to assert running-timer continuity, a scope expansion that was not part of the assigned flake fix and that duplicated behavior already being added in a follow-up commit. That unrelated test rewrite means the scoped fix commit was not limited to stabilizing readSessionBootstrap polling.

issues.description: This flaky-fix feature was scoped to stabilizing readSessionBootstrap polling after workspace switch, but the same commit also rewrote VAL-CROSS-005 into a running-timer continuity scenario with new setup, assertions, and cleanup. That broad behavior/test expansion is unrelated to the bootstrap 500 flake and overlaps follow-up work that landed immediately afterward, so the feature commit does not stay scoped to the requested stabilization change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-test-worker skill tells workers to use agent-browser for manual verification unless browser-only risk is absent or tooling conflicts block it, but the reviewed transcript completed without any browser-tool attempt and without recording a blocker or rationale in the handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file .factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md step 9 requires agent-browser or a recorded exception; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 73a1e049-5714-415f-ab21-fd3577c7ab24 shows only file/tool/test commands and the handoff's interactiveChecks array is empty.

summary: Fail: the commit contains the intended pollSessionBootstrap stabilization, but it also folds in a larger VAL-CROSS-005 test rewrite outside the requested flake-fix scope, so the reviewed feature is not cleanly limited to the assigned session-bootstrap fix.
